name: va_range_lemma_typo_j_unconstrained
reason: va_range_lemma has a copy-paste bug — the ensures says `0 <= i < len && 0 <= i < len` (duplicated `i`) instead of `0 <= j < len`, so j is completely unconstrained, making the injectivity claim hold for arbitrary out-of-range j values

name: va_add_range_nonzero_distinct
reason: va_add_range's external_body ensures `i != 0 ==> ret != va` without overflow checks — but spec_va_add_range computes `(va + i*4096) as usize` which can wrap to equal va, so the trusted ensures is unsound for large inputs

name: va_range_lemma_out_of_bounds_injectivity
reason: Due to the `0 <= i < len` typo (j not bounded), the lemma claims injectivity between in-range index i and out-of-range index len — this could be false if spec_va_add_range wraps around

name: va_4k_range_valid_vacuous_zero_len
reason: spec_va_4k_range_valid with len==0 is vacuously true for ANY va including invalid addresses — no va_4k_valid precondition needed

name: va_range_lemma_derives_false_for_overflow
reason: The j-unconstrained typo lets va_range_lemma assert injectivity for j=usize::MAX — this is trusted external_body claiming a property about arithmetic overflow that may not hold, potentially introducing unsoundness
